本单元入口
整套首页
unit_05 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_05

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 397 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize
braggartreducecause
braggart: standard unit headword | reduce: standard unit headword | cause: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional
confessionalanonymous
confessional: standard unit headword | anonymous: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
Some species camouflage patterns may be __________, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.Some species camouflage patterns may be __________, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.
答案：A　plastic
patternheterogeneous
pattern: standard unit headword | heterogeneous: standard unit headword
#4 SE
In spite of her fascination with chances, coincidence, and contingency, Stanning’s novels are __________ constructed, written from the head rather than the heart.In spite of her fascination with chances, coincidence, and contingency, Stanning’s novels are __________ constructed, written from the head rather than the heart.
答案：C, D　painstakingly; meticulously
coincidencecontingency
coincidence: standard unit headword | contingency: standard unit headword
#5 TC TRIPLE
Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　aping; instructive; escaped
doomparadigm
doom: standard unit headword | paradigm: standard unit headword
#6 TC SINGLE
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart
dispassionatestalwart
dispassionate: standard unit headword | stalwart: standard unit headword
#7 TC DOUBLE
Intuitively, something is considered to be matter if, and only if, there is mass along with energy and/or momentum associated with it. In other words, possession of mass along with energy and/or momentum is regarded as (i) __________, rather than (ii) __________, property of matter.Intuitively, something is considered to be matter if, and only if, there is mass along with energy and/or momentum associated with it. In other words, possession of mass along with energy and/or momentum is regarded as (i) __________, rather than (ii) __________, property of matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an essential; an accidental
irreduciblecontingent
irreducible: standard unit headword | contingent: standard unit headword
#8 SE
Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.
答案：C, E　more sluggish; slower
versatileadaptable
versatile: standard unit headword | adaptable: standard unit headword
#9 SE
The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright __________.The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright __________.
答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
patronizeantediluvian
patronize: standard unit headword | antediluvian: standard unit headword
#10 TC SINGLE
Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?
答案：E　exculpation
vindicationvalediction
vindication: standard unit headword | valediction: standard unit headword

Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　dogmatic; qualify; plentiful
anachronisticfusty
anachronistic: standard unit headword | fusty: standard unit headword
#12 SE
In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chuang __________ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chuang __________ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.
答案：B, C　conceded; acknowledged
coincideconcede
coincide: standard unit headword | concede: standard unit headword
#13 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
formidablesuspect
formidable: standard unit headword | suspect: standard unit headword
#14 SE
Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.
答案：D, E　supplant; supersede
complementpromise
complement: standard unit headword | promise: standard unit headword
#15 SE
Every illness is a story, and when Annie’s began it was characterized by the kinds of __________ details that mean nothing until seen in hindsight.Every illness is a story, and when Annie’s began it was characterized by the kinds of __________ details that mean nothing until seen in hindsight.
答案：B, F　unexceptional; nondescript
salientnondescript
salient: standard unit headword | nondescript: standard unit headword
#16 TC DOUBLE
Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)__________ philosophy, (ii)__________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)__________ philosophy, (ii)__________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an anomalous; embodies
discretion
discretion: standard unit headword
#17 TC DOUBLE
Proffering one increasingly improbable scene and character after another, (i)__________ by the constraint of realism, the novel revels in this (ii)__________ by ever more brazenly defying its readers’ presumed expectations.Proffering one increasingly improbable scene and character after another, (i)__________ by the constraint of realism, the novel revels in this (ii)__________ by ever more brazenly defying its readers’ presumed expectations.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　untrammeled; implausibility
revel
revel: standard unit headword

This is neither praise nor criticism, neither a compliment nor __________, just an observation.This is neither praise nor criticism, neither a compliment nor __________, just an observation.
答案：C　an animadversion
compliment
compliment: standard unit headword
#19 TC SINGLE
In interviews, it is easy to mistake the novelist’s silences for aloofness, especially since in the court of public opinion all writers are guilty of being __________ until proven innocent.In interviews, it is easy to mistake the novelist’s silences for aloofness, especially since in the court of public opinion all writers are guilty of being __________ until proven innocent.
答案：A　snobs
snob
snob: standard unit headword
#20 TC TRIPLE
Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored
correspondence
correspondence: standard unit headword
#21 TC DOUBLE
Each new generation of students grows up (i)__________ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality, that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii)__________.Each new generation of students grows up (i)__________ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality, that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism
alien
alien: standard unit headword
#22 TC DOUBLE
Researchers note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)__________: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)__________ the top cur.Researchers note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)__________: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)__________ the top cur.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow
umbrage
umbrage: standard unit headword
#23 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
legal
legal: standard unit headword
#24 TC SINGLE
Even though the authors repeatedly __________ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.Even though the authors repeatedly __________ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.
答案：B　extol
far-fetched
far-fetched: standard unit headword

In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot
patron
patron: standard unit headword

He was so __________ during the filming of the movie that crew members nicknamed him "the angriest man in the world”.He was so __________ during the filming of the movie that crew members nicknamed him "the angriest man in the world”.
答案：B　irascible
irascible
irascible: standard unit headword
#27 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
chronology
chronology: standard unit headword

She was never (i) __________; she was nothing if not discreet, so she (ii) __________ for the present to declare her passion.She was never (i) __________; she was nothing if not discreet, so she (ii) __________ for the present to declare her passion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　precipitate; forbore
discreet
discreet: standard unit headword

The researcher noted that microbes, though __________, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.The researcher noted that microbes, though __________, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.
答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular
diminutive
diminutive: standard unit headword
#30 TC TRIPLE
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
uncover
uncover: standard unit headword

Though many avant-garde writers __________ traditional distinctions among literary categories, combining elements of biography and fiction, prose and poetry, this fusion of forms has been slow to catch on with publishers.Though many avant-garde writers __________ traditional distinctions among literary categories, combining elements of biography and fiction, prose and poetry, this fusion of forms has been slow to catch on with publishers.
答案：A　flout
fusion
fusion: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　arcane; obscure

答案：A, F　accession to; acquiescence to

#34 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
ancillary
ancillary: standard unit headword
#35 TC DOUBLE
The strategists who created the European Unions practiced piecemeal social engineering. Recognizing that perfection is (i)__________, they set limited objectives and then mobilize the political will for a small step forward knowing full well that when they achieved it, its (ii)__________ would become apparent and necessitate further measures.The strategists who created the European Unions practiced piecemeal social engineering. Recognizing that perfection is (i)__________, they set limited objectives and then mobilize the political will for a small step forward knowing full well that when they achieved it, its (ii)__________ would become apparent and necessitate further measures.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unattainable; inadequacy
mobilize
mobilize: standard unit headword

答案：C　numinous
numinous

The laboratory maze has grown ever less __________ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.The laboratory maze has grown ever less __________ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.
答案：A, B　intricate; convoluted

Blank 2
Blank 3

Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.

Blank 2

答案：C, D　gratify; please
amalgam

It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace
alienation

Blank 2

答案：E　propitious

#45 SE
In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always __________.In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always __________.
答案：A, C　porous; permeable
hard-and-fast
hard-and-fast: standard unit headword
#46 TC TRIPLE
In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy; understandably, space in such a book is (i)__________, and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book's value had he more effectively (ii)__________ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy; understandably, space in such a book is (i)__________, and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book's value had he more effectively (ii)__________ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of
premium
premium: standard unit headword

The kilt has certainly had (i) __________ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii) __________ from men's to women's apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.The kilt has certainly had (i) __________ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii) __________ from men's to women's apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　a contentious; reclassified
ridicule

Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more __________, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more __________, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.
答案：B　pristine
parochialheterogeneous

Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive
restive

Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated
suspicious
suspicious: standard unit headword
#51 TC DOUBLE
As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) __________ increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) __________.As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) __________ increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　conviviality; anxiety
boisterous
boisterous: standard unit headword
#52 TC SINGLE
The actor's performance was so absurdly __________ that Gwen felt a little ashamed to have to resort to tissues in the final scene.The actor's performance was so absurdly __________ that Gwen felt a little ashamed to have to resort to tissues in the final scene.
答案：D　maudlin
maudlin
maudlin: standard unit headword
#53 TC TRIPLE
Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　presence; function as a proxy for; supporting
discrete
discrete: standard unit headword
#54 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
sensational
sensational: standard unit headword
#55 TC DOUBLE
Mr. Billington, at times, can be a (i)__________. For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company’s eight-and-a-half hour version of Nicholas Nickleby, which many found (ii)__________.Mr. Billington, at times, can be a (i)__________. For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company’s eight-and-a-half hour version of Nicholas Nickleby, which many found (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　naysayer; absorbing
braggartpushover
braggart: standard unit headword | pushover: standard unit headword
#56 TC SINGLE
The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.
答案：C　profligate
profligate
profligate: standard unit headword
#57 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
theatrical
theatrical: standard unit headword
#58 SE
The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.
答案：A, B　unostentatious; modest
majestic
majestic: standard unit headword
#59 TC DOUBLE
Managers who categorically squelch insights from low-tiered employees run the obvious hazard of (i) __________ creativity; conversely, these very same managers are more likely to (ii) __________ any ideas that flow down from the top brass.Managers who categorically squelch insights from low-tiered employees run the obvious hazard of (i) __________ creativity; conversely, these very same managers are more likely to (ii) __________ any ideas that flow down from the top brass.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　smothering; unquestioningly embrace

答案：A　an autonomous
enervate
enervate: standard unit headword
#61 TC TRIPLE
Fifteen years ago, the author of this scholarly monograph would have (i)__________ found a university press willing to publish it. But today specialized books of this sort are (ii)__________ species. Their main purchasers - university libraries - have far less money to spend on these items than they once did. Computerized catalogues, subscription content, expensive scientific journals, exploding storage costs: all these demands are (iii)__________ library budgets.Fifteen years ago, the author of this scholarly monograph would have (i)__________ found a university press willing to publish it. But today specialized books of this sort are (ii)__________ species. Their main purchasers - university libraries - have far less money to spend on these items than they once did. Computerized catalogues, subscription content, expensive scientific journals, exploding storage costs: all these demands are (iii)__________ library budgets.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　readily; an imperiled; putting tremendous pressure on

cause: standard unit headword | appropriation: standard unit headword
#62 TC DOUBLE
Stigler brings a fundamental (i) __________ to his protagonists, somehow treating them with respect even while he reveals these sometimes prickly characters behaving in (ii) __________ ways.Stigler brings a fundamental (i) __________ to his protagonists, somehow treating them with respect even while he reveals these sometimes prickly characters behaving in (ii) __________ ways.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　sympathy; petulant
petulant
petulant: standard unit headword
#63 SE
Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting
trendy
trendy: standard unit headword
#64 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
persevere
persevere: standard unit headword
#65 TC DOUBLE
It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)__________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)__________.It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)__________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection
theatrics
theatrics: standard unit headword

Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani's book managed to (i)__________ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)__________ some of the (iii)__________ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani's book managed to (i)__________ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)__________ some of the (iii)__________ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile
puncture
puncture: standard unit headword

The current __________ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.The current __________ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.
答案：A, D　glut; surfeit

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability

The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)__________ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)__________ striking mental (iii)__________.The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)__________ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)__________ striking mental (iii)__________.

答案：B, D, I　unaware of; belies; lapses

答案：B, D　scathing; vitriolic

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　mercurial; inconstancy

答案：E　promise

答案：B, F　decline; health

To many newspaper readers now, this book published in 1953 is (i)__________. It is filled with references to some people whose ideas seemed (ii)__________ at that time, but are rarely viewed as (iii)__________ persons now.To many newspaper readers now, this book published in 1953 is (i)__________. It is filled with references to some people whose ideas seemed (ii)__________ at that time, but are rarely viewed as (iii)__________ persons now.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　somewhat dated; important; seminal

For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
braggadocio
braggadocio: standard unit headword

This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　wrangling; frisson

答案：A　specious
formidable

An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

答案：C　enigmatic

答案：A, C　prejudicial; tendentious

答案：B　fusion
fusion

#83 SE
In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Reggie Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Reggie Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　extremist; zealot
patron

#84 TC SINGLE
One baffling aspect of the novel is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most elementary __________: readers must accept not an occasional coincidence, but a continuous stream of them.One baffling aspect of the novel is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most elementary __________: readers must accept not an occasional coincidence, but a continuous stream of them.
答案：E　credibility
coincidence

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　familiar; revisionist

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword
#87 SE
Far from __________ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.Far from __________ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.
答案：A, E　spurring; fostering

In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.
答案：B　extirpate

Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

There is (i)__________ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)__________, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)__________ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.There is (i)__________ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)__________, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)__________ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　little; major; corresponding to

答案：B　zealous

#101 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#102 SE
An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar
heterogeneous
heterogeneous: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
So (i)__________ is the reputation of the city's police force for (ii)__________ that whenever a new police chief takes office, he or she routinely promises to clean up the force.So (i)__________ is the reputation of the city's police force for (ii)__________ that whenever a new police chief takes office, he or she routinely promises to clean up the force.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　persistent; corruption
promise
promise: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
legal
legal: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more __________ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more __________ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.
答案：D, F　visible; salient
salient
salient: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i)__________. Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would be bogged down in the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i)__________. Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would be bogged down in the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　an advocate; analysis of events
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would __________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would __________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter
alienation
alienation: standard unit headword
#109 SE
William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：A, F　fiery; impassioned
impassioned
impassioned: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)__________. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)__________. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)__________ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)__________. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)__________. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)__________ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Collecting such fragment of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i)__________ artifacts into objective (ii)__________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.Collecting such fragment of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i)__________ artifacts into objective (ii)__________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　trivial; importance
pattern
pattern: standard unit headword
#112 TC SINGLE
For a small group of experts, the field is a lifelong obsession, but it is so __________ that it mystifies virtually everyone else.For a small group of experts, the field is a lifelong obsession, but it is so __________ that it mystifies virtually everyone else.
答案：A　arcane
arcane
arcane: standard unit headword
#113 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
paradigm
paradigm: standard unit headword
#114 SE
To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.
答案：C, E　paltry; meager
compliment
compliment: standard unit headword
#115 TC DOUBLE
Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking
coincidence
coincidence: standard unit headword
#116 TC SINGLE
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart
dispassionatestalwart
dispassionate: standard unit headword | stalwart: standard unit headword
#117 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#118 SE
Writing about advances in climate science is often problematic, in part because the material is so __________: climate science is the study of shifting, interrelated, and sometimes paradoxical patterns.Writing about advances in climate science is often problematic, in part because the material is so __________: climate science is the study of shifting, interrelated, and sometimes paradoxical patterns.
答案：C, E　complicated; knotty
pattern
pattern: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
Up to the 1970s, historians of science tended to be (i)__________, not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii)__________ in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitive on such scores.Up to the 1970s, historians of science tended to be (i)__________, not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii)__________ in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitive on such scores.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　anachronistic; major
anachronistic
anachronistic: standard unit headword
#120 TC SINGLE
The depletion of soil and trees in southern West Virginia has resulted in frequent flash floods, since when the mountain slopes are __________ storms cause torrents of water to pour down them unimpeded.The depletion of soil and trees in southern West Virginia has resulted in frequent flash floods, since when the mountain slopes are __________ storms cause torrents of water to pour down them unimpeded.
答案：C　denuded
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#121 SE
Some kinds of deadly bacteria, including those that cause tetanus, tuberculosis, and botulism, remain __________ until something triggers their insidious activity.Some kinds of deadly bacteria, including those that cause tetanus, tuberculosis, and botulism, remain __________ until something triggers their insidious activity.
答案：A, B　harmless; innocuous
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#122 TC TRIPLE
China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed
doom
doom: standard unit headword
#123 TC DOUBLE
There has been great enthusiasm in the United States for reducing fossil fuel dependence by increasing production biofuels from crops such as corn and switchgrass, but this (i)__________ about biofuels’ potential should be (ii)__________ by a realistic appraisal of the costs and challenges of biofuel production.There has been great enthusiasm in the United States for reducing fossil fuel dependence by increasing production biofuels from crops such as corn and switchgrass, but this (i)__________ about biofuels’ potential should be (ii)__________ by a realistic appraisal of the costs and challenges of biofuel production.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　exuberance; tempered
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword
#124 TC SINGLE
The process in which Earth's crust rises or sinks is not __________: the movement varies from place to place and has occurred irregularly as a result of different causes.The process in which Earth's crust rises or sinks is not __________: the movement varies from place to place and has occurred irregularly as a result of different causes.
答案：A　linear
cause
cause: standard unit headword

Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#126 SE
A few decades ago the idea of animal morality would have been met with __________; however，recent research suggests that animals not only act altruistically but also have the capacity for empathy, forgiveness, trust, and reciprocity.A few decades ago the idea of animal morality would have been met with __________; however，recent research suggests that animals not only act altruistically but also have the capacity for empathy, forgiveness, trust, and reciprocity.
答案：A, E　derision; ridicule
ridicule
ridicule: standard unit headword
#127 TC DOUBLE
Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i) __________ philosophy, (ii) __________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas: that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and given a measure of discretion over their own learning.Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i) __________ philosophy, (ii) __________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas: that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and given a measure of discretion over their own learning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an anomalous; embodies
discretion

Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.
答案：D　inhibit
cause
cause: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure

While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.
答案：B　a panacea

#134 TC TRIPLE
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

Blank 1
Blank 2

The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.
答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

答案：D　variegated
metaphor

Barker’s account of how morality have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)__________, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.Barker’s account of how morality have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)__________, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable

As human interactions become (ii) __________ physical location, people are less likely to have regular dealings with others who do not share the same values and outlooks.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　provincialism; less contingent on

答案：A　alacrity
legal

答案：A, B　abound; proliferate

答案：A, E　spurring; fostering

But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

The book is a determinedly apolitical writer's attempt to deal with an explosive subject, and some readers will think it too __________; they will miss the impassioned engagement found in the works of her peers.The book is a determinedly apolitical writer's attempt to deal with an explosive subject, and some readers will think it too __________; they will miss the impassioned engagement found in the works of her peers.
答案：E　measured

#149 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　obtain; glean

#152 TC TRIPLE
There are two opposing theories about mountain formation and climate over the past 40 million years: either the surge of mountain building (i)__________ the global cooling or vice versa. The first of these two theories asserts that widespread mountain building cooled the earth as a result of the (ii)__________ mountains and climate. For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)__________: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.There are two opposing theories about mountain formation and climate over the past 40 million years: either the surge of mountain building (i)__________ the global cooling or vice versa. The first of these two theories asserts that widespread mountain building cooled the earth as a result of the (ii)__________ mountains and climate. For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)__________: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　caused; feedback between; self-perpetuating

cause: standard unit headword
#153 TC SINGLE
The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.
答案：B　unwarranted

答案：B, D　audacious; striking

True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii) __________ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettisons; ignoring

Blank 2

答案：C　recede

答案：B, D　artificial; spurious

At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.Excitement is often considered anathema in science writing because it interjects the scientist's emotion and values into the work. Indeed, one should not be too quick to (i)__________ of science. At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　abandon the objectivity; dispassionate; misleading

答案：C　mendacity

答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii) __________ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i) __________. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. 答案：B, C　stave off; forestall

Blank 2

答案：B, E　ancillary to; subsidiary to

(i) __________ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii) __________ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt a player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players' blood.Scientists suspect that athletes may experience augmented bloodstream levels of the hormone oxytocin during competition. (i) __________ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii) __________ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt a player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players' blood.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　practical; quantify changes in
suspect
suspect: standard unit headword

But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic

Therefore, studies of population declines that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Blank 2

答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
